name: Tenant Rollout & Change Enablement

scenarios.title: Create a durable tenant rollout packet
steps: Open the Sprint 11 Tenant Rollout & Change Enablement workspace.
Review tenant users, org units, roles, admin controls, rollout factory packets, AI quality release packets, audit events, and action queue signals.
expected: A durable AssistantTenantRolloutPacket row is created with tenant profile, stakeholder map, rollout waves, enablement plan, communication plan, adoption telemetry, support model, cutover checklist, rollback plan, response plan, and leadership summary JSON.
An AssistantAuditEvent records that packet creation is evidence-only.
No tenant, user, role, org unit, module flag, training assignment, communication, seed command, launch state, or tenant setting is changed automatically.

scenarios.title: Queue change board review
steps: Add an optional tenant rollout review note to a draft Sprint 11 packet.
expected: The packet moves to CHANGE_REVIEW_QUEUED and stores actionQueueItemId.
An AssistantActionQueueItem is created for tenant_rollout_change_review with the packet URL and rollout evidence.
The queued review is separate from launch execution; the assistant does not invite users, grant roles, enable modules, send communications, run seeds, or change rollout mode automatically.

scenarios.title: Approve reviewed rollout evidence
steps: Review stakeholder gaps, rollout waves, enablement modules, communications, adoption telemetry, support model, cutover blockers, and rollback plan.
expected: The packet status becomes APPROVED with approvedAt, approvedByUserId, and approvalNote.
An AssistantAuditEvent records approval and reiterates the no-silent-change guardrail.

Approval records rollout governance evidence only; actual invitations, role grants, module changes, communications, seed runs, and tenant setting updates require separate downstream approved workflows.

guardrails: No tenant is created, cloned, renamed, launched, archived, or deleted automatically.
No user is invited, activated, deactivated, moved between waves, or assigned training automatically.
No role, permission, org unit, support owner, or stakeholder assignment is created or changed automatically.
No module flag, rollout mode, runtime setting, seed command, connector credential, or tenant setting is changed automatically.
No email, notification, announcement, customer communication, training invite, or support ticket is sent automatically.
No rollout factory packet, AI quality release packet, admin control, audit event, or action queue item is silently approved or closed.
